However, in such an arrangement, interest can also include consideration for other basic lending risks (for example, liquidity risk) and costs (for example, administrative costs) associated with holding the financial asset for a particular period of time. In addition, interest can include a profit margin that is consistent with a basic lending arrangement. In extreme economic circumstances, interest can be negative if, for example, the holder of a financial asset either explicitly or implicitly pays for the deposit of its money for a particular period of time (and that fee exceeds the consideration that the holder receives for the time value of money, credit risk and other basic lending risks and costs). **However, contractual terms that introduce exposure to risks or volatility in the contractual cash flows that is unrelated to a basic lending arrangement, such as exposure to changes in equity prices or commodity prices, do not give rise to contractual cash flows that are solely payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding.** An originated or a purchased financial asset can be a basic lending arrangement irrespective of whether it is a loan in its legal form.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B4.1.14\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"The following examples illustrate contractual cash flows that are **not solely payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding.** This list of examples is not exhaustive.\\\\nInstrumentFInstrumentFisabondthatisconvertibleintoafixednumberofequityinstrumentsoftheissuer.The holder would analyse the convertible bond in its entirety.\\\\n**The contractual cash flows are not payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding because they reflect a return that is inconsistent with a basic lending arrangement** (see paragraph B4.1.7A); ie the return is linked to the value of the equity of the issuer.\\\\nInstrumentGInstrumentGisaloanthatpaysaninversefloatinginterestrate(ietheinterestratehasaninverserelationshiptomarketinterestrates).**The contractual cash flows are not solely payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding.**\\\\nThe interest amounts are not consideration for the time value of money on the principal amount outstanding.\\\\nInstrumentHInstrumentHisaperpetualinstrumentbuttheissuermaycalltheinstrumentatanypointandpaytheholdertheparamountplusaccruedinterestdue.InstrumentHpaysamarketinterestratebutpaymentofinterestcannotbemadeunlesstheissuerisabletoremainsolventimmediatelyafterwards.Deferredinterestdoesnotaccrueadditionalinterest.**The contractual cash flows are not payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding.** That is because the issuer may be required to defer interest payments and additional interest does not accrue on those deferred interest amounts. As a result, interest amounts are not consideration for the time value of money on the principal amount outstanding.\\\\nIf interest accrued on the deferred amounts, the contractual cash flows could be payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding.\\\\nThe fact that Instrument H is perpetual does not in itself mean that the contractual cash flows are not payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding. In effect, a perpetual instrument has continuous (multiple) extension options. Such options may result in contractual cash flows that are payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding if interest payments are mandatory and must be paid in perpetuity.\\\\nAlso, the fact that Instrument H is callable does not mean that the contractual cash flows are not payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding unless it is callable at an amount that does not substantially reflect payment of outstanding principal and interest on that principal amount outstanding. Even if the callable amount includes an amount that reasonably compensates the holder for the early termination of the instrument, the contractual cash flows could be payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding. (See also paragraph B4.1.12.)\\\\nInstrumentIInstrumentIisaloanwithaninterestratethatisadjustedeveryreportingperiodtotrackthemovementsinamarket-determinedcarbonpriceindexduringtheprecedingreportingperiod.**The contractual cash flows are not solely payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding.**\\\\nThe contractual cash flows are indexed to a variable (the carbon price index), which is not a basic lending risk or cost. The contractual cash flows are therefore inconsistent with a basic lending arrangement (see paragraph B4.1.8A).\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B4.1.16\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"This may be the case if the financial asset represents an investment in particular assets or cash flows and hence **the contractual cash flows are not solely payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding.** For example, if the contractual terms stipulate that the financial asset’s cash flows increase as more automobiles use a particular toll road, those contractual cash flows are inconsistent with a basic lending arrangement. **As a result, the instrument would not satisfy the condition in paragraphs 4.1.2(b) and 4.1.2A(b).**\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    }\\n  ]\\n}\"}","expires":1778853335044}

cache.value: {"value":"{\"output\":\"{\\n  \\\"assumptions_fr\\\": [\\n    \\\"On suppose que l’instrument en cause est bien un actif financier au sens d’IAS 32 avant d’appliquer IFRS 9.\\\",\\n    \\\"On suppose que la question vise la qualification d’un actif financier satisfaisant au critère SPPI, sans informations suffisantes sur le modèle économique de détention.\\\",\\n    \\\"On suppose qu’aucune désignation volontaire à la juste valeur par résultat au titre de l’option de juste valeur n’est précisée dans les faits.\\\"\\n  ],\\n  \\\"recommendation\\\": {\\n    \\\"answer\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n    \\\"justification\\\": \\\"Un actif financier respectant le critère SPPI est un actif dont les flux contractuels sont, à des dates spécifiées, uniquement des remboursements de principal et des intérêts sur le principal restant dû. En revanche, sa catégorie comptable n’est pas déterminée par le seul critère SPPI : elle dépend aussi du modèle économique selon IFRS 9.\\\"\\n  },\\n  \\\"approaches\\\": [\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_1\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"amortised_cost\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Coût amorti\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Dans cette situation, le seul fait que l’actif respecte SPPI ne suffit pas pour conclure au coût amorti. IFRS 9 exige aussi qu’il soit détenu dans un modèle dont l’objectif est d’encaisser les flux contractuels (IFRS 9 4.1.2(a)), en plus de flux constitués uniquement de principal et d’intérêts (IFRS 9 4.1.2(b), 4.1.3).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"L’actif est détenu dans un modèle économique visant à percevoir les flux contractuels.\\\",\\n        \\\"Les flux contractuels à dates spécifiées sont uniquement des remboursements de principal et des intérêts sur le principal restant dû.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Si ces conditions sont remplies, l’actif est classé et évalué ultérieurement au coût amorti.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"4.1.2\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"A financial asset shall be measured at amortised cost if both of the following conditions are met: **(a)the financial asset is held within a business model whose objective is to hold financial assets in order to collect contractual cash flows and (b)the contractual terms of the financial asset give rise on specified dates to cash flows that are solely payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding.** Paragraphs B4.1.1⁠–⁠B4.1.26 provide guidance on how to apply these conditions.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"4.1.3\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"For the purpose of applying paragraphs 4.1.2(b) and 4.1.2A(b) : **(a)principal is the fair value of the financial asset at initial recognition.** Paragraph B4.1.7B provides additional guidance on the meaning of principal. **(b)interest consists of consideration for the time value of money, for the credit risk associated with the principal amount outstanding during a particular period of time and for other basic lending risks and costs, as well as a profit margin.** Paragraphs B4.1.7A and B4.1.9A⁠–⁠B4.1.9E provide additional guidance on the meaning of interest, including the meaning of the time value of money.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_2\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"fair_value_through_other_comprehensive_income\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Juste valeur par autres éléments du résultat global\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Dans cette situation, un actif respectant SPPI peut relever de la JVOCI si le modèle économique est à la fois d’encaisser les flux contractuels et de vendre les actifs (IFRS 9 4.1.2A(a)). Le critère SPPI reste requis et renvoie à la notion de principal et d’intérêts définie par IFRS 9 4.1.3.\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"L’actif est détenu dans un modèle économique dont l’objectif est à la fois d’encaisser les flux contractuels et de vendre les actifs financiers.\\\",\\n        \\\"Les flux contractuels à dates spécifiées sont uniquement des remboursements de principal et des intérêts sur le principal restant dû.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Si ces conditions sont remplies, l’actif est classé et évalué ultérieurement à la juste valeur par autres éléments du résultat global.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"4.1.2A\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"A financial asset shall be measured at fair value through other comprehensive income if both of the following conditions are met: **(a)the financial asset is held within a business model whose objective is achieved by both collecting contractual cash flows and selling financial assets and (b)the contractual terms of the financial asset give rise on specified dates to cash flows that are solely payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding.** Paragraphs B4.1.1⁠–⁠B4.1.26 provide guidance on how to apply these conditions.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"4.1.3\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"For the purpose of applying paragraphs 4.1.2(b) and 4.1.2A(b) : **(a)principal is the fair value of the financial asset at initial recognition.** Paragraph B4.1.7B provides additional guidance on the meaning of principal. **(b)interest consists of consideration for the time value of money, for the credit risk associated with the principal amount outstanding during a particular period of time and for other basic lending risks and costs, as well as a profit margin.** Paragraphs B4.1.7A and B4.1.9A⁠–⁠B4.1.9E provide additional guidance on the meaning of interest, including the meaning of the time value of money.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_3\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"fair_value_through_profit_or_loss\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Juste valeur par résultat\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Dans cette situation, la JVR ne constitue pas la définition de l’actif SPPI, mais la catégorie résiduelle si l’actif n’entre ni au coût amorti ni à la JVOCI (IFRS 9 4.1.4). Ainsi, même avec SPPI, cette approche s’applique si le modèle économique ne correspond à aucune des deux catégories précédentes, ou en cas de désignation spécifique à la JVR selon l’approche identifiée.\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"Le modèle économique ne correspond ni à un objectif de détention pour encaisser, ni à un objectif de collecte et de vente.\\\",\\n        \\\"Ou l’actif est désigné à la juste valeur par résultat conformément à l’option prévue par IFRS 9.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"À défaut de classement au coût amorti ou à la JVOCI, l’actif est évalué ultérieurement à la juste valeur par résultat.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"4.1.4\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"A financial asset shall be measured at fair value through profit or loss **unless it is measured at amortised cost in accordance with paragraph 4.1.2 or at fair value through other comprehensive income in accordance with paragraph 4.1.2A.** However an entity may make an irrevocable election at initial recognition for particular investments in equity instruments that would otherwise be measured at fair value through profit or loss to present subsequent changes in fair value in other comprehensive income (see paragraphs 5.7.5⁠–⁠5.7.6). E13, E14\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    }\\n  ]\\n}\"}","expires":1778853376447}

cache.value: {"value":"{\"output\":\"{\\n  \\\"assumptions_fr\\\": [\\n    \\\"La question vise un actif financier déjà qualifié d'actif financier au sens d'IAS 32 et porte uniquement sur sa définition au regard du critère SPPI d'IFRS 9.\\\",\\n    \\\"Aucun fait complémentaire n'est fourni sur le modèle de gestion de l'entité ni sur une éventuelle désignation à la juste valeur par résultat à l'origine.\\\"\\n  ],\\n  \\\"recommendation\\\": {\\n    \\\"answer\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n    \\\"justification\\\": \\\"On peut définir l'actif comme un instrument de dette dont les flux contractuels sont uniquement des remboursements de principal et des intérêts, c'est-à-dire des flux compatibles avec un prêt de base. Toutefois, en IFRS 9, cette caractéristique ne suffit pas seule à fixer sa catégorie comptable, qui dépend aussi du business model et, le cas échéant, d'une désignation en JV résultat.\\\"\\n  },\\n  \\\"approaches\\\": [\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_1\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"amortised_cost\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Coût amorti\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Dans cette situation, le respect du critère SPPI permet seulement d'entrer dans le champ possible du coût amorti, mais ne suffit pas à lui seul. IFRS 9 exige aussi que l'actif soit détenu dans un modèle visant à collecter les flux contractuels (IFRS 9 4.1.2), les flux SPPI correspondant à un \\\\\\\"basic lending arrangement\\\\\\\" (IFRS 9 B4.1.7A).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"L'actif doit être détenu dans un modèle économique dont l'objectif est de collecter les flux contractuels.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Si cette condition de modèle économique est remplie, l'actif SPPI peut être classé au coût amorti.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"4.1.2\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"A financial asset shall be measured at amortised cost if both of the following conditions are met: **(a)the financial asset is held within a business model whose objective is to hold financial assets in order to collect contractual cash flows and (b)the contractual terms of the financial asset give rise on specified dates to cash flows that are solely payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding.** Paragraphs B4.1.1⁠–⁠B4.1.26 provide guidance on how to apply these conditions.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B4.1.7A\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Contractual cash flows that are solely payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding are **consistent with a basic lending arrangement**. In a basic lending arrangement, consideration for the time value of money (see paragraphs B4.1.9A⁠–⁠B4.1.9E) and credit risk are typically the most significant elements of interest. However, in such an arrangement, interest can also include consideration for other basic lending risks (for example, liquidity risk) and costs (for example, administrative costs) associated with holding the financial asset for a particular period of time. In addition, interest can include a profit margin that is consistent with a basic lending arrangement. In extreme economic circumstances, interest can be negative if, for example, the holder of a financial asset either explicitly or implicitly pays for the deposit of its money for a particular period of time (and that fee exceeds the consideration that the holder receives for the time value of money, credit risk and other basic lending risks and costs). However, contractual terms that introduce exposure to risks or volatility in the contractual cash flows that is unrelated to a basic lending arrangement, such as exposure to changes in equity prices or commodity prices, **do not give rise to contractual cash flows that are solely payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding**. An originated or a purchased financial asset can be a basic lending arrangement irrespective of whether it is a loan in its legal form.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_2\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"fair_value_through_other_comprehensive_income\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Juste valeur par autres éléments du résultat global\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Dans cette situation, un actif respectant SPPI peut aussi relever de la JV par OCI si l'objectif du portefeuille est à la fois de collecter les flux et de vendre les actifs (IFRS 9 4.1.2A). Le critère SPPI reste défini par des paiements de principal et d'intérêts sur le principal restant dû (IFRS 9 4.1.3).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"L'actif doit être détenu dans un modèle économique dont l'objectif est à la fois de collecter les flux contractuels et de vendre les actifs.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Si ce modèle mixte est démontré, l'actif SPPI peut être classé en JV par OCI.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"4.1.2A\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"A financial asset shall be measured at fair value through other comprehensive income if both of the following conditions are met: **(a)the financial asset is held within a business model whose objective is achieved by both collecting contractual cash flows and selling financial assets and (b)the contractual terms of the financial asset give rise on specified dates to cash flows that are solely payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding.** Paragraphs B4.1.1⁠–⁠B4.1.26 provide guidance on how to apply these conditions.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"4.1.3\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"For the purpose of applying paragraphs 4.1.2(b) and 4.1.2A(b) : **(a)principal is the fair value of the financial asset at initial recognition.** Paragraph B4.1.7B provides additional guidance on the meaning of principal. **(b)interest consists of consideration for the time value of money, for the credit risk associated with the principal amount outstanding during a particular period of time and for other basic lending risks and costs, as well as a profit margin.** Paragraphs B4.1.7A and B4.1.9A⁠–⁠B4.1.9E provide additional guidance on the meaning of interest, including the meaning of the time value of money.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_3\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"fair_value_through_profit_or_loss\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Juste valeur par résultat\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Dans cette situation, même si l'actif respecte SPPI, il peut relever de la JV résultat soit parce qu'il ne satisfait pas aux conditions de coût amorti ou de JV par OCI (IFRS 9 4.1.4), soit parce qu'il est irrévocablement désigné ainsi à l'origine pour réduire une incohérence comptable (IFRS 9 4.1.5). Le seul critère SPPI ne donne donc pas, à lui seul, la catégorie finale.\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"L'actif n'est pas détenu dans un modèle éligible au coût amorti ou à la JV par OCI.\\\",\\n        \\\"Ou l'entité exerce à l'origine une désignation irrévocable en JV résultat pour éliminer ou réduire significativement une incohérence comptable.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Un actif SPPI n'est pas nécessairement hors JV résultat ; cette catégorie demeure résiduelle ou optionnelle par désignation.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"4.1.4\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"A financial asset shall be measured at fair value through profit or loss **unless it is measured at amortised cost in accordance with paragraph 4.1.2 or at fair value through other comprehensive income in accordance with paragraph 4.1.2A**. However an entity may make an irrevocable election at initial recognition for particular investments in equity instruments that would otherwise be measured at fair value through profit or loss to present subsequent changes in fair value in other comprehensive income (see paragraphs 5.7.5⁠–⁠5.7.6). E13, E14\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"4.1.5\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Despite paragraphs 4.1.1⁠–⁠4.1.4, **an entity may, at initial recognition, irrevocably designate a financial asset as measured at fair value through profit or loss if doing so eliminates or significantly reduces a measurement or recognition inconsistency** (sometimes referred to as an ‘accounting mismatch’) that would otherwise arise from measuring assets or liabilities or recognising the gains and losses on them on different bases (see paragraphs B4.1.29⁠–⁠B4.1.32).\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    }\\n  ]\\n}\"}","expires":1778853396960}

Il sera en coût amorti ou en FVOCI si le modèle économique correspondant est rempli ; sinon, ou par désignation, il sera en FVPL.\\\"\\n  },\\n  \\\"approaches\\\": [\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_1\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"amortised_cost\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Coût amorti\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Dans cette situation, le seul fait que les flux soient uniquement du principal et des intérêts permet de satisfaire le critère contractuel SPPI, mais cela ne suffit pas à lui seul pour retenir le coût amorti. Le coût amorti n’est applicable que si l’actif est aussi détenu dans un modèle économique visant à encaisser les flux contractuels (IFRS 9 4.1.2 ; IFRS 9 4.1.3).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"L’actif est détenu dans un modèle économique dont l’objectif est de détenir les actifs pour en percevoir les flux contractuels.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Si cette condition de modèle économique est remplie, l’actif peut être classé et évalué ultérieurement au coût amorti.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"4.1.2\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"A financial asset shall be measured at amortised cost if both of the following conditions are met: **(a)the specified dates to cash flows that are solely payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding.** Paragraphs B4.1.1⁠–⁠B4.1.26 provide guidance on how to apply these conditions.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"4.1.3\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"For the purpose of applying paragraphs 4.1.2(b) and 4.1.2A(b) : **(a)principal is the fair value of the financial asset at initial recognition.** Paragraph B4.1.7B provides additional guidance on the meaning of principal. **(b)interest consists of consideration for the time value of money, for the credit risk associated with the principal amount outstanding during a particular period of time and for other basic lending risks and costs, as well as a profit margin.** Paragraphs B4.1.7A and B4.1.9A⁠–⁠B4.1.9E provide additional guidance on the meaning of interest, including the meaning of the time value of money.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_2\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"fair_value_through_other_comprehensive_income\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Juste valeur par autres éléments du résultat global\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Dans cette situation, le critère SPPI est compatible avec une qualification en FVOCI, mais uniquement si le modèle économique consiste à la fois à encaisser les flux contractuels et à vendre les actifs financiers. Les flux de principal et d’intérêts sont donc nécessaires, mais la conclusion dépend encore du modèle de gestion (IFRS 9 4.1.2A ; IFRS 9 4.1.1).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"L’actif est détenu dans un modèle économique dont l’objectif est atteint à la fois par la collecte des flux contractuels et par la vente d’actifs financiers.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Si ce modèle économique mixte est démontré, l’actif peut être classé et évalué ultérieurement en FVOCI.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"4.1.2A\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"A financial asset shall be measured at fair value through other comprehensive income if both of the following conditions are met: **(a)the financial asset is held within a business model whose objective is achieved by both collecting contractual cash flows and selling financial assets and (b)the contractual terms of the financial asset give rise on specified dates to cash flows that are solely payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding.** Paragraphs B4.1.1⁠–⁠B4.1.26 provide guidance on how to apply these conditions.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"4.1.1\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Unless paragraph 4.1.5 applies, an entity shall classify financial assets as subsequently measured at amortised cost, fair value through other comprehensive income or fair value through profit or loss on the basis of both: **(a)the entity’s business model for managing the financial assets and (b)the contractual cash flow characteristics of the financial asset.**\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_3\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"fair_value_through_profit_or_loss\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Juste valeur par résultat\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Dans cette situation, un actif pourtant SPPI peut quand même relever de la FVPL si les conditions du coût amorti ou du FVOCI ne sont pas remplies, car la FVPL est la catégorie résiduelle (IFRS 9 4.1.4). En outre, même si l’actif est SPPI, une désignation irrévocable en FVPL est possible à l’origine pour éliminer ou réduire significativement un mismatch comptable (IFRS 9 4.1.5).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"Le modèle économique ne remplit pas les conditions du coût amorti ni celles du FVOCI.\\\",\\n        \\\"Ou l’entité désigne irrévocablement l’actif en FVPL à l’origine pour éliminer ou réduire significativement une incohérence comptable.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"À défaut des modèles économiques ouvrant droit au coût amorti ou au FVOCI, ou en cas de désignation, l’actif est évalué ultérieurement en FVPL.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"4.1.4\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"A financial asset shall be measured at fair value through profit or loss **unless it is measured at amortised cost in accordance with paragraph 4.1.2 or at fair value through other comprehensive income in accordance with paragraph 4.1.2A.** However an entity may make an irrevocable election at initial recognition for particular investments in equity instruments that would otherwise be measured at fair value through profit or loss to present subsequent changes in fair value in other comprehensive income (see paragraphs 5.7.5⁠–⁠5.7.6). E13, E14\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"4.1.5\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Despite paragraphs 4.1.1⁠–⁠4.1.4, **an entity may, at initial recognition, irrevocably designate a financial asset as measured at fair value through profit or loss if doing so eliminates or significantly reduces a measurement or recognition inconsistency** (sometimes referred to as an ‘accounting mismatch’) that would otherwise arise from measuring assets or liabilities or recognising the gains and losses on them on different bases (see paragraphs B4.1.29⁠–⁠B4.1.32).\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    }\\n  ]\\n}\"}","expires":1778853403208}

cache.value: {"value":"{\"output\":\"{\\n  \\\"assumptions_fr\\\": [\\n    \\\"On suppose qu’il s’agit d’un actif financier entrant dans le champ d’IFRS 9.\\\",\\n    \\\"On suppose que le seul fait connu est que les flux contractuels sont uniquement des paiements de principal et d’intérêts sur le principal restant dû.\\\",\\n    \\\"On suppose que le modèle économique de gestion de l’actif et l’éventuelle désignation à la juste valeur par résultat pour éviter un décalage comptable ne sont pas précisés dans la question.\\\"\\n  ],\\n  \\\"recommendation\\\": {\\n    \\\"answer\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n    \\\"justification\\\": \\\"En IFRS 9, un actif ayant uniquement des flux de principal et d’intérêts satisfait le critère SPPI, mais cela ne suffit pas à lui seul pour fixer sa qualification finale. Sa classification dépend ensuite du modèle économique: coût amorti, JVOCI ou, à défaut ou par désignation, JVR.\\\"\\n  },\\n  \\\"approaches\\\": [\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_1\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"amortised_cost\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Coût amorti\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Dans cette situation, le critère de flux uniquement constitués de principal et d’intérêts est compatible avec le coût amorti, mais IFRS 9 exige aussi un modèle économique de détention pour encaisser les flux contractuels (IFRS 9 4.1.2). La seule présence de flux SPPI ne permet donc pas, à elle seule, de conclure au coût amorti (IFRS 9 4.1.1, 4.1.3).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"L’actif doit être détenu dans un modèle économique dont l’objectif est de détenir les actifs pour en percevoir les flux contractuels.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Si cette condition de modèle économique est remplie, l’actif peut être classé au coût amorti.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"4.1.1\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Unless paragraph 4.1.5 applies, an entity shall classify financial assets as subsequently measured at amortised cost, fair value through other comprehensive income or fair value through profit or loss on the basis of both: **(a)the entity’s business model for managing the financial assets and (b)the contractual cash flow characteristics of the financial asset.**\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"4.1.2\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"A financial asset shall be measured at amortised cost if both of the following conditions are met: **(a)the financial asset is held within a business model whose objective is to hold financial assets in order to collect contractual cash flows and (b)the contractual terms of the financial asset give rise on specified dates to cash flows that are solely payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding.** Paragraphs B4.1.1⁠–⁠B4.1.26 provide guidance on how to apply these conditions.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"4.1.3\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"For the purpose of applying paragraphs 4.1.2(b) and 4.1.2A(b) : **(a)principal is the fair value of the financial asset at initial recognition.** Paragraph B4.1.7B provides additional guidance on the meaning of principal. **(b)interest consists of consideration for the time value of money, for the credit risk associated with the principal amount outstanding during a particular period of time and for other basic lending risks and costs, as well as a profit margin.** Paragraphs B4.1.7A and B4.1.9A⁠–⁠B4.1.9E provide additional guidance on the meaning of interest, including the meaning of the time value of money.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_2\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"fair_value_through_other_comprehensive_income\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Juste valeur par autres éléments du résultat global\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Dans cette situation, le critère SPPI est aussi compatible avec la JVOCI, mais seulement si le modèle économique combine encaissement des flux contractuels et ventes d’actifs financiers (IFRS 9 4.1.2A). Comme la question ne précise pas ce modèle économique, cette qualification reste conditionnelle malgré la présence de flux SPPI (IFRS 9 4.1.1, 4.1.3).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"L’actif doit être détenu dans un modèle économique dont l’objectif est atteint à la fois par l’encaissement des flux contractuels et par la vente d’actifs financiers.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Si ce modèle économique est démontré, l’actif peut être classé à la juste valeur par OCI.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"4.1.1\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Unless paragraph 4.1.5 applies, an entity shall classify financial assets as subsequently measured at amortised cost, fair value through other comprehensive income or fair value through profit or loss on the basis of both: **(a)the entity’s business model for managing the financial assets and (b)the contractual cash flow characteristics of the financial asset.**\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"4.1.2A\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"A financial asset shall be measured at fair value through other comprehensive income if both of the following conditions are met: **(a)the financial asset is held within a business model whose objective is achieved by both collecting contractual cash flows and selling financial assets and (b)the contractual terms of the financial asset give rise on specified dates to cash flows that are solely payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding.** Paragraphs B4.1.1⁠–⁠B4.1.26 provide guidance on how to apply these conditions.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"4.1.3\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"For the purpose of applying paragraphs 4.1.2(b) and 4.1.2A(b) : **(a)principal is the fair value of the financial asset at initial recognition.** Paragraph B4.1.7B provides additional guidance on the meaning of principal. **(b)interest consists of consideration for the time value of money, for the credit risk associated with the principal amount outstanding during a particular period of time and for other basic lending risks and costs, as well as a profit margin.** Paragraphs B4.1.7A and B4.1.9A⁠–⁠B4.1.9E provide additional guidance on the meaning of interest, including the meaning of the time value of money.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_3\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"fair_value_through_profit_or_loss\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Juste valeur par résultat\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Dans cette situation, la JVR reste la catégorie résiduelle dès lors que l’actif n’est pas classé au coût amorti ou en JVOCI faute d’informations suffisantes sur le modèle économique (IFRS 9 4.1.4). En outre, même si l’actif satisfait le critère SPPI, une désignation irrévocable en JVR peut être faite à l’origine pour éliminer ou réduire significativement un décalage comptable (IFRS 9 4.1.5).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"Une désignation en JVR n’est possible qu’à la comptabilisation initiale et seulement si elle élimine ou réduit significativement un décalage comptable.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"À défaut de qualification démontrée au coût amorti ou en JVOCI, ou en cas de désignation valable, l’actif est classé à la juste valeur par résultat.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"4.1.4\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"A financial asset shall be measured at fair value through profit or loss **unless it is measured at amortised cost in accordance with paragraph 4.1.2 or at fair value through other comprehensive income in accordance with paragraph 4.1.2A.** However an entity may make an irrevocable election at initial recognition for particular investments in equity instruments that would otherwise be measured at fair value through profit or loss to present subsequent changes in fair value in other comprehensive income (see paragraphs 5.7.5⁠–⁠5.7.6). E13, E14\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"4.1.5\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Despite paragraphs 4.1.1⁠–⁠4.1.4, **an entity may, at initial recognition, irrevocably designate a financial asset as measured at fair value through profit or loss if doing so eliminates or significantly reduces a measurement or recognition inconsistency** (sometimes referred to as an ‘accounting mismatch’) that would otherwise arise from measuring assets or liabilities or recognising the gains and losses on them on different bases (see paragraphs B4.1.29⁠–⁠B4.1.32).\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    }\\n  ]\\n}\"}","expires":1778853449938}

cache.value: {"value":"{\"output\":\"{\\n  \\\"assumptions_fr\\\": [\\n    \\\"La question est comprise comme une demande de définition générale du critère SPPI (« solely payments of principal and interest ») d’IFRS 9, sans instrument ni modèle de gestion précis.\\\",\\n    \\\"Aucune option de désignation supplémentaire ni fait particulier (convertibilité, indexation complexe, non-recours, clause conditionnelle spécifique) n’est fourni au-delà de la question de principe.\\\"\\n  ],\\n  \\\"recommendation\\\": {\\n    \\\"answer\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n    \\\"justification\\\": \\\"Un actif financier génère exclusivement des paiements de principal et d’intérêts si ses clauses contractuelles ne produisent, aux dates spécifiées, que du principal et une rémunération cohérente avec un prêt de base. S’il existe une exposition contractuelle à des risques ou variables non compatibles avec un prêt de base, le critère SPPI n’est pas rempli et la mesure résiduelle est à la JV par résultat.\\\"\\n  },\\n  \\\"approaches\\\": [\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_1\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"amortised_cost\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Coût amorti\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Dans cette situation, le coût amorti n’est possible que si l’actif satisfait au test SPPI et s’il est détenu dans un modèle visant à encaisser les flux contractuels (IFRS 9 4.1.2). Le sens de SPPI est donné par IFRS 9 4.1.3 et B4.1.7A : le principal correspond à la juste valeur initiale, et l’intérêt rémunère le temps, le risque de crédit, d’autres risques/coûts de prêt de base et une marge.\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"L’actif est détenu dans un modèle économique dont l’objectif est de détenir les actifs pour en percevoir les flux contractuels.\\\",\\n        \\\"Les clauses contractuelles donnent lieu, à des dates spécifiées, uniquement à des paiements de principal et d’intérêts sur le principal restant dû.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Si ces deux conditions sont réunies, la catégorie de mesure pertinente est le coût amorti.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"4.1.2\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"A financial asset shall be measured at amortised cost if both of the following conditions are met: **(a)the financial asset is held within a business model whose objective is to hold financial assets in order to collect contractual cash flows and (b)the contractual terms of the financial asset give rise on specified dates to cash flows that are solely payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding.** Paragraphs B4.1.1⁠–⁠B4.1.26 provide guidance on how to apply these conditions.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"4.1.3\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"For the purpose of applying paragraphs 4.1.2(b) and 4.1.2A(b) : **(a)principal is the fair value of the financial asset at initial recognition.** Paragraph B4.1.7B provides additional guidance on the meaning of principal. **(b)interest consists of consideration for the time value of money, for the credit risk associated with the principal amount outstanding during a particular period of time and for other basic lending risks and costs, as well as a profit margin.** Paragraphs B4.1.7A and B4.1.9A⁠–⁠B4.1.9E provide additional guidance on the meaning of interest, including the meaning of the time value of money.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B4.1.7A\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"**Contractual cash flows that are solely payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding are consistent with a basic lending arrangement.** In a basic lending arrangement, consideration for the time value of money (see paragraphs B4.1.9A⁠–⁠B4.1.9E) and credit risk are typically the most significant elements of interest. However, in such an arrangement, interest can also include consideration for other basic lending risks (for example, liquidity risk) and costs (for example, administrative costs) associated with holding the financial asset for a particular period of time. **In addition, interest can include a profit margin**... However, **contractual terms that introduce exposure to risks or volatility in the contractual cash flows that is unrelated to a basic lending arrangement, such as exposure to changes in equity prices or commodity prices, do not give rise to contractual cash flows that are solely payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding.**\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_2\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"fair_value_through_other_comprehensive_income\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Juste valeur par autres éléments du résultat global\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Dans cette situation, la JVOCI n’est envisageable que si le même test SPPI est satisfait, mais avec un modèle économique combinant encaissement des flux contractuels et vente (IFRS 9 4.1.2A). L’analyse des flux reste identique : ils doivent refléter un prêt de base, sans indexation ou levier incompatibles (IFRS 9 B4.1.7 et B4.1.8A).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"L’actif est détenu dans un modèle économique dont l’objectif est atteint à la fois par l’encaissement des flux contractuels et par la vente d’actifs financiers.\\\",\\n        \\\"Les clauses contractuelles donnent lieu, à des dates spécifiées, uniquement à des paiements de principal et d’intérêts sur le principal restant dû.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Si le critère SPPI est rempli et que le modèle économique combine encaissement et vente, la catégorie pertinente est la JVOCI.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"4.1.2A\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"A financial asset shall be measured at fair value through other comprehensive income if both of the following conditions are met: **(a)the financial asset is held within a business model whose objective is achieved by both collecting contractual cash flows and selling financial assets and (b)the contractual terms of the financial asset give rise on specified dates to cash flows that are solely payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding.** Paragraphs B4.1.1⁠–⁠B4.1.26 provide guidance on how to apply these conditions.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B4.1.7\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"**Paragraph 4.1.1(b) requires an entity to classify a financial asset on the basis of its contractual cash flow characteristics** if the financial asset is held within a business model whose objective is to hold assets to collect contractual cash flows or within a business model whose objective is achieved by both collecting contractual cash flows and selling financial assets, unless paragraph 4.1.5 applies. **To do so, the condition in paragraphs 4.1.2(b) and 4.1.2A(b) requires an entity to determine whether the asset’s contractual cash flows are solely payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding.**\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B4.1.8A\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"In assessing whether the contractual cash flows of a financial asset are consistent with a basic lending arrangement, an entity may have to consider the different elements of interest separately. The assessment of interest focuses on what an entity is being compensated for, rather than how much compensation an entity receives. Nonetheless, the amount of compensation an entity receives may indicate that the entity is being compensated for something other than basic lending risks and costs. **Contractual cash flows are inconsistent with a basic lending arrangement if they are indexed to a variable that is not a basic lending risk or cost (for example, the value of equity instruments or the price of a commodity) or if they represent a share of the debtor’s revenue or profit**, even if such contractual terms are common in the market in which the entity operates.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_3\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"fair_value_through_profit_or_loss\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Juste valeur par résultat\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Pour répondre à la question, la JV par résultat est la conséquence lorsque les clauses contractuelles ne satisfont pas le sens de SPPI. IFRS 9 4.1.4 impose cette catégorie par défaut, et IFRS 9 B4.1.9 et B4.1.14 donnent des exemples d’échec du test, notamment le levier, la convertibilité en actions ou une indexation incompatible avec un prêt de base.\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Si les flux contractuels incluent autre chose que principal et intérêts de prêt de base, l’actif relève de la JV par résultat.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"4.1.4\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"**A financial asset shall be measured at fair value through profit or loss unless** it is measured at amortised cost in accordance with paragraph 4.1.2 or at fair value through other comprehensive income in accordance with paragraph 4.1.2A. However an entity may make an irrevocable election at initial recognition for particular investments in equity instruments that would otherwise be measured at fair value through profit or loss to present subsequent changes in fair value in other comprehensive income (see paragraphs 5.7.5⁠–⁠5.7.6). E13, E14\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B4.1.9\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"**Leverage is a contractual cash flow characteristic of some financial assets. Leverage increases the variability of the contractual cash flows with the result that they do not have the economic characteristics of interest.** Stand‑alone option, forward and swap contracts are examples of financial assets that include such leverage. **Thus, such contracts do not meet the condition in paragraphs 4.1.2(b) and 4.1.2A(b) and cannot be subsequently measured at amortised cost or fair value through other comprehensive income.**\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B4.1.14\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"The following examples illustrate contractual cash flows that are **not solely payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding**. This list of examples is not exhaustive. InstrumentF Instrument F is a bond that is convertible into a fixed number of equity instruments of the issuer. The holder would analyse the convertible bond in its entirety. **The contractual cash flows are not payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding because they reflect a return that is inconsistent with a basic lending arrangement** (see paragraph B4.1.7A); ie the return is linked to the value of the equity of the issuer. InstrumentG Instrument G is a loan that pays an inverse floating interest rate (ie the interest rate has an inverse relationship to market interest rates). **The contractual cash flows are not solely payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding.** The interest amounts are not consideration for the time value of money on the principal amount outstanding... InstrumentI Instrument I is a loan with an interest rate that is adjusted every reporting period to track the movements in a market-determined carbon price index during the preceding reporting period. **The contractual cash flows are not solely payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding.** The contractual cash flows are indexed to a variable (the carbon price index), which is not a basic lending risk or cost. The contractual cash flows are therefore inconsistent with a basic lending arrangement (see paragraph B4.1.8A).\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    }\\n  ]\\n}\"}","expires":1778853485464}

cache.value: {"value":"{\"output\":\"{\\n  \\\"assumptions_fr\\\": [\\n    \\\"La question est comprise comme une demande de définition du critère SPPI (« solely payments of principal and interest ») en IFRS 9, et non comme l’analyse d’un instrument précis.\\\",\\n    \\\"Faute de faits contractuels spécifiques, l’applicabilité des catégories de mesure est appréciée de manière conditionnelle selon le respect ou non du test SPPI et selon le modèle économique.\\\"\\n  ],\\n  \\\"recommendation\\\": {\\n    \\\"answer\\\": \\\"oui\\\",\\n    \\\"justification\\\": \\\"Selon IFRS 9, un actif financier génère exclusivement des paiements de principal et d’intérêts si ses flux contractuels correspondent à un prêt de base: principal = juste valeur initiale, intérêts = contrepartie de la valeur temps de l’argent, du risque de crédit, d’autres risques/coûts de base et d’une marge. Toute exposition contractuelle à des prix d’actions, de matières premières ou à un levier fait échouer le test SPPI.\\\"\\n  },\\n  \\\"approaches\\\": [\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_1\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"amortised_cost\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Coût amorti\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Dans cette situation, le coût amorti n’est pertinent que si l’actif satisfait d’abord au test SPPI puis est détenu dans un modèle économique de conservation pour encaisser les flux contractuels (IFRS 9 4.1.2). Le sens de SPPI est donné par IFRS 9 4.1.3 et B4.1.7A: les flux doivent être ceux d’un prêt de base et ne pas exposer le porteur à des risques sans lien avec un prêt de base, comme des prix d’actions ou de matières premières.\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"L’actif est détenu dans un modèle économique visant à encaisser les flux contractuels.\\\",\\n        \\\"Les clauses contractuelles donnent lieu, à des dates spécifiées, uniquement à des paiements de principal et d’intérêts.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Si ces conditions sont remplies, la catégorie de mesure peut être le coût amorti.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"4.1.2\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"A financial asset shall be measured at amortised cost if both of the following conditions are met: (a)the financial asset is held within a business model whose objective is to hold financial assets in order to collect contractual cash flows and (b)the contractual terms of the financial asset **give rise on specified dates to cash flows that are solely payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding**. Paragraphs B4.1.1⁠–⁠B4.1.26 provide guidance on how to apply these conditions.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"4.1.3\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"For the purpose of applying paragraphs 4.1.2(b) and 4.1.2A(b) : (a)**principal is the fair value of the financial asset at initial recognition**. Paragraph B4.1.7B provides additional guidance on the meaning of principal. (b)**interest consists of consideration for the time value of money, for the credit risk associated with the principal amount outstanding during a particular period of time and for other basic lending risks and costs, as well as a profit margin**. Paragraphs B4.1.7A and B4.1.9A⁠–⁠B4.1.9E provide additional guidance on the meaning of interest, including the meaning of the time value of money.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B4.1.7A\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Contractual cash flows that are **solely payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding are consistent with a basic lending arrangement**. In a basic lending arrangement, consideration for the time value of money (see paragraphs B4.1.9A⁠–⁠B4.1.9E) and credit risk are typically the most significant elements of interest. However, in such an arrangement, interest can also include consideration for other basic lending risks (for example, liquidity risk) and costs (for example, administrative costs) associated with holding the financial asset for a particular period of time. In addition, interest can include a profit margin that is consistent with a basic lending arrangement. In extreme economic circumstances, interest can be negative if, for example, the holder of a financial asset either explicitly or implicitly pays for the deposit of its money for a particular period of time (and that fee exceeds the consideration that the holder receives for the time value of money, credit risk and other basic lending risks and costs). **However, contractual terms that introduce exposure to risks or volatility in the contractual cash flows that is unrelated to a basic lending arrangement, such as exposure to changes in equity prices or commodity prices, do not give rise to contractual cash flows that are solely payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding.** An originated or a purchased financial asset can be a basic lending arrangement irrespective of whether it is a loan in its legal form.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_2\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"fair_value_through_other_comprehensive_income\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Juste valeur par autres éléments du résultat global\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Dans cette situation, cette catégorie n’est possible que si le test SPPI est satisfait et si le modèle économique consiste à la fois à encaisser les flux contractuels et à vendre les actifs (IFRS 9 4.1.2A). La notion de SPPI reste la même: flux d’un prêt de base, sans indexation à des variables non basiques comme un indice actions ou le prix d’une matière première (IFRS 9 B4.1.8A).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"L’actif est détenu dans un modèle économique combinant encaissement des flux contractuels et ventes.\\\",\\n        \\\"Les clauses contractuelles donnent lieu, à des dates spécifiées, uniquement à des paiements de principal et d’intérêts.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Si ces conditions sont remplies, la catégorie de mesure peut être la JV par OCI.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"4.1.2A\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"A financial asset shall be measured at fair value through other comprehensive income if both of the following conditions are met: (a)the financial asset is held within a business model whose objective is achieved by both collecting contractual cash flows and selling financial assets and (b)the contractual terms of the financial asset **give rise on specified dates to cash flows that are solely payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding**. Paragraphs B4.1.1⁠–⁠B4.1.26 provide guidance on how to apply these conditions.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B4.1.8A\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"In assessing whether the contractual cash flows of a financial asset are consistent with a basic lending arrangement, an entity may have to consider the different elements of interest separately. The assessment of interest focuses on what an entity is being compensated for, rather than how much compensation an entity receives. Nonetheless, the amount of compensation an entity receives may indicate that the entity is being compensated for something other than basic lending risks and costs. **Contractual cash flows are inconsistent with a basic lending arrangement if they are indexed to a variable that is not a basic lending risk or cost (for example, the value of equity instruments or the price of a commodity)** or if they represent a share of the debtor’s revenue or profit, even if such contractual terms are common in the market in which the entity operates.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_3\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"fair_value_through_profit_or_loss\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Juste valeur par résultat\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Dans cette situation, la JV par résultat devient la conclusion si l’actif échoue au test SPPI. IFRS 9 indique qu’un levier contractuel, ou une exposition à des variables étrangères à un prêt de base, comme un prix de matière première, empêche de conclure à des paiements uniquement de principal et d’intérêts (IFRS 9 B4.1.9, B4.1.14).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"Les flux contractuels ne satisfont pas au test SPPI ou l’actif ne remplit pas les conditions des autres catégories.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Si le test SPPI échoue, l’actif relève en pratique de la JV par résultat.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B4.1.9\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"**Leverage is a contractual cash flow characteristic of some financial assets. Leverage increases the variability of the contractual cash flows with the result that they do not have the economic characteristics of interest.** Stand‑alone option, forward and swap contracts are examples of financial assets that include such leverage. **Thus, such contracts do not meet the condition in paragraphs 4.1.2(b) and 4.1.2A(b) and cannot be subsequently measured at amortised cost or fair value through other comprehensive income.**\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B4.1.14\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"The following examples illustrate contractual cash flows that are not solely payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding. This list of examples is not exhaustive. InstrumentFInstrumentFisabondthatisconvertibleintoafixednumberofequityinstrumentsoftheissuer.The holder would analyse the convertible bond in its entirety. **The contractual cash flows are not payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding because they reflect a return that is inconsistent with a basic lending arrangement** (see paragraph B4.1.7A); ie the return is linked to the value of the equity of the issuer. InstrumentGInstrumentGisaloanthatpaysaninversefloatinginterestrate(ietheinterestratehasaninverserelationshiptomarketinterestrates). **The contractual cash flows are not solely payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding.** The interest amounts are not consideration for the time value of money on the principal amount outstanding. InstrumentHInstrumentHisaperpetualinstrumentbuttheissuermaycalltheinstrumentatanypointandpaytheholdertheparamountplusaccruedinterestdue.InstrumentHpaysamarketinterestratebutpaymentofinterestcannotbemadeunlesstheissuerisabletoremainsolventimmediatelyafterwards.Deferredinterestdoesnotaccrueadditionalinterest. **The contractual cash flows are not payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding.** That is because the issuer may be required to defer interest payments and additional interest does not accrue on those deferred interest amounts. As a result, interest amounts are not consideration for the time value of money on the principal amount outstanding. If interest accrued on the deferred amounts, the contractual cash flows could be payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding. The fact that Instrument H is perpetual does not in itself mean that the contractual cash flows are not payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding. In effect, a perpetual instrument has continuous (multiple) extension options. Such options may result in contractual cash flows that are payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding if interest payments are mandatory and must be paid in perpetuity. Also, the fact that Instrument H is callable does not mean that the contractual cash flows are not payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding unless it is callable at an amount that does not substantially reflect payment of outstanding principal and interest on that principal amount outstanding. Even if the callable amount includes an amount that reasonably compensates the holder for the early termination of the instrument, the contractual cash flows could be payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding. (See also paragraph B4.1.12.) InstrumentIInstrumentIisaloanwithaninterestratethatisadjustedeveryreportingperiodtotrackthemovementsinamarket-determinedcarbonpriceindexduringtheprecedingreportingperiod. **The contractual cash flows are not solely payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding.** The contractual cash flows are indexed to a variable (the carbon price index), which is not a basic lending risk or cost. The contractual cash flows are therefore inconsistent with a basic lending arrangement (see paragraph B4.1.8A).\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    }\\n  ]\\n}\"}","expires":1778853491786}

Comment définit-on précisément ce type d’actif dont les termes contractuels ne prévoient que ces paiements ?:{"id":"exec:uv run python /Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/scripts/run_answer.py","config":{"policy-config":"./effective/policy.default.yaml","llm_provider":"openai-codex","basePath":"/Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/experiments/51_answer-evals_Q3/runs/2026-05-01_13-49-24_promptfoo-eval-family-q3"},"env":{}}

cache.value: {"value":"{\"output\":\"{\\n  \\\"assumptions_fr\\\": [\\n    \\\"La question est interprétée comme portant sur la définition IFRS 9 du critère SPPI (« solely payments of principal and interest »), sans autre fait spécifique sur le modèle économique de détention.\\\",\\n    \\\"On suppose que l’actif examiné est un actif financier relevant du champ de classement d’IFRS 9.\\\"\\n  ],\\n  \\\"recommendation\\\": {\\n    \\\"answer\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n    \\\"justification\\\": \\\"IFRS 9 définit précisément un actif à flux contractuels limités au principal et aux intérêts : les termes doivent générer, à des dates spécifiées, uniquement des paiements de principal et d’intérêts au sens d’IFRS 9. La catégorie de mesure en résultant dépend ensuite du modèle économique de gestion de l’actif.\\\"\\n  },\\n  \\\"approaches\\\": [\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_1\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"amortised_cost\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Coût amorti\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Dans cette situation, le coût amorti n’est possible que si l’actif répond à la définition SPPI et s’il est détenu dans un modèle économique visant à encaisser les flux contractuels (IFRS 9 4.1.2). La définition précise du SPPI repose sur des flux, à des dates spécifiées, constitués uniquement de principal et d’intérêts; le principal est la juste valeur initiale et l’intérêt rémunère le temps, le risque de crédit et d’autres risques/coûts de prêt de base (IFRS 9 4.1.3, B4.1.7A).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"L’actif est détenu dans un modèle économique dont l’objectif est de détenir les actifs pour en percevoir les flux contractuels.\\\",\\n        \\\"Les termes contractuels génèrent, à des dates spécifiées, uniquement des paiements de principal et d’intérêts au sens d’IFRS 9.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Si ces deux conditions sont réunies, l’actif peut être classé au coût amorti.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"4.1.2\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"A financial asset shall be measured at amortised cost if both of the following conditions are met: (a) the financial asset is held within a business model whose objective is to hold financial assets in order to collect contractual cash flows and (b) **the contractual terms of the financial asset give rise on specified dates to cash flows that are solely payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding.** Paragraphs B4.1.1–B4.1.26 provide guidance on how to apply these conditions.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"4.1.3\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"For the purpose of applying paragraphs 4.1.2(b) and 4.1.2A(b) : (a) **principal is the fair value of the financial asset at initial recognition.** Paragraph B4.1.7B provides additional guidance on the meaning of principal. (b) **interest consists of consideration for the time value of money, for the credit risk associated with the principal amount outstanding during a particular period of time and for other basic lending risks and costs, as well as a profit margin.** Paragraphs B4.1.7A and B4.1.9A–B4.1.9E provide additional guidance on the meaning of interest, including the meaning of the time value of money.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B4.1.7A\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"**Contractual cash flows that are solely payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding are consistent with a basic lending arrangement.** In a basic lending arrangement, consideration for the time value of money (see paragraphs B4.1.9A–B4.1.9E) and credit risk are typically the most significant elements of interest. However, in such an arrangement, interest can also include consideration for other basic lending risks (for example, liquidity risk) and costs (for example, administrative costs) associated with holding the financial asset for a particular period of time. In addition, interest can include a profit margin that is consistent with a basic lending arrangement.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_2\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"fair_value_through_other_comprehensive_income\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Juste valeur par autres éléments du résultat global\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Dans cette situation, la JV par OCI s’applique seulement si l’actif satisfait d’abord la définition SPPI, puis s’il est détenu dans un modèle dont l’objectif est à la fois de collecter les flux contractuels et de vendre les actifs (IFRS 9 4.1.2A). La définition SPPI reste identique : principal = juste valeur initiale; intérêts = rémunération d’un prêt de base, sans exposition à des risques non basiques (IFRS 9 4.1.3, B4.1.7).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"L’actif est détenu dans un modèle économique combinant encaissement des flux contractuels et vente.\\\",\\n        \\\"Les termes contractuels génèrent, à des dates spécifiées, uniquement des paiements de principal et d’intérêts au sens d’IFRS 9.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Si ces conditions sont remplies, l’actif peut être classé à la juste valeur par OCI.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"4.1.2A\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"A financial asset shall be measured at fair value through other comprehensive income if both of the following conditions are met: (a) the financial asset is held within a business model whose objective is achieved by both collecting contractual cash flows and selling financial assets and (b) **the contractual terms of the financial asset give rise on specified dates to cash flows that are solely payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding.** Paragraphs B4.1.1–B4.1.26 provide guidance on how to apply these conditions.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"4.1.3\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"For the purpose of applying paragraphs 4.1.2(b) and 4.1.2A(b) : (a) **principal is the fair value of the financial asset at initial recognition.** Paragraph B4.1.7B provides additional guidance on the meaning of principal. (b) **interest consists of consideration for the time value of money, for the credit risk associated with the principal amount outstanding during a particular period of time and for other basic lending risks and costs, as well as a profit margin.** Paragraphs B4.1.7A and B4.1.9A–B4.1.9E provide additional guidance on the meaning of interest, including the meaning of the time value of money.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B4.1.7\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Paragraph 4.1.1(b) requires an entity to classify a financial asset on the basis of its contractual cash flow characteristics if the financial asset is held within a business model whose objective is to hold assets to collect contractual cash flows or within a business model whose objective is achieved by both collecting contractual cash flows and selling financial assets, unless paragraph 4.1.5 applies. To do so, **the condition in paragraphs 4.1.2(b) and 4.1.2A(b) requires an entity to determine whether the asset’s contractual cash flows are solely payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding.**\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_3\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"fair_value_through_profit_or_loss\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Juste valeur par résultat\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Dans cette situation, la JV par résultat devient la catégorie résiduelle lorsque l’actif n’entre pas dans un modèle « encaisser » ni « encaisser et vendre », ou lorsqu’il ne satisfait pas au test SPPI (IFRS 9 4.1.1, B4.1.5). Ainsi, même un actif dont les flux sont limités au principal et aux intérêts peut être en JV par résultat si le modèle économique est fondé sur la juste valeur ou la vente active (IFRS 9 B4.1.6).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"Le modèle économique n’est ni « détenir pour encaisser » ni « détenir pour encaisser et vendre ».\\\",\\n        \\\"Ou bien les termes contractuels ne satisfont pas au critère SPPI.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"À défaut de satisfaire la combinaison modèle économique + SPPI requise pour les autres catégories, l’actif est classé en juste valeur par résultat.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"4.1.1\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Unless paragraph 4.1.5 applies, an entity shall classify financial assets as subsequently measured at amortised cost, fair value through other comprehensive income or fair value through profit or loss on the basis of both: (a) the entity’s business model for managing the financial assets and (b) **the contractual cash flow characteristics of the financial asset.**\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B4.1.5\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"**Financial assets are measured at fair value through profit or loss if they are not held within a business model whose objective is to hold assets to collect contractual cash flows or within a business model whose objective is achieved by both collecting contractual cash flows and selling financial assets** (but see also paragraph 5.7.5). One business model that results in measurement at fair value through profit or loss is one in which an entity manages the financial assets with the objective of realising cash flows through the sale of the assets.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B4.1.6\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"A portfolio of financial assets that is managed and whose performance is evaluated on a fair value basis (as described in paragraph 4.2.2(b)) is neither held to collect contractual cash flows nor held both to collect contractual cash flows and to sell financial assets. The entity is primarily focused on fair value information and uses that information to assess the assets’ performance and to make decisions. In addition, a portfolio of financial assets that meets the definition of held for trading is not held to collect contractual cash flows or held both to collect contractual cash flows and to sell financial assets. For such portfolios, the collection of contractual cash flows is only incidental to achieving the business model’s objective. **Consequently, such portfolios of financial assets must be measured at fair value through profit or loss.**\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    }\\n  ]\\n}\"}","expires":1778853541335}
